title: ficus disticha subsp. calodictya
intro: Ficus disticha subsp. calodictya, also known as the Calodictyon tree or the Two-tiered Fig, is a subspecies of the Ficus disticha tree, native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ficus disticha subsp. calodictya include reducing fever, treating wounds and skin conditions, and providing relief from digestive issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
